---
title: "R Episode 5 — Exploring Data Frames"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Objectives
By the end of this episode you will be able to:

- Import a CSV into a tibble with `readr`.
- Inspect dimensions, columns, and types.
- Get quick summaries of a dataset.

**Teaching:** 30 min · **Exercises:** 20 min
:::

## Data frames and tibbles

A **data frame** is R's table: columns are vectors (each one type), rows are observations. The
tidyverse's **tibble** is a modern data frame with nicer printing and no surprise coercions.

We'll use the **gapminder** dataset throughout this track.

```r
library(readr)
library(here)

gapminder <- read_csv(here("data", "gapminder.csv"))
# Or, if you installed the package:  gapminder <- gapminder::gapminder
gapminder
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Instructor note
`read_csv()` prints the column types it guessed. Pause on this — checking that types are right
*at import time* prevents downstream bugs (e.g. a year read as character).
:::

## Inspecting structure

```r
dim(gapminder)        # rows, columns
nrow(gapminder); ncol(gapminder)
names(gapminder)      # column names
head(gapminder)       # first rows
str(gapminder)        # types + preview
glimpse(gapminder)    # dplyr's transposed, readable view
```

## Quick summaries

```r
summary(gapminder)             # per-column summary stats
unique(gapminder$continent)    # distinct values of a column
length(unique(gapminder$country))
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Challenge — First questions
Without any new packages, answer:

1. How many rows and columns does `gapminder` have?
2. What type is the `year` column?
3. How many distinct continents are present?

### Solution

```r
dim(gapminder)
class(gapminder$year)
length(unique(gapminder$continent))
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Key points
- `read_csv()` imports data and reports column types — verify them early.
- A tibble is a friendly data frame; columns are typed vectors.
- `glimpse()`, `str()`, and `summary()` are your first look at any dataset.
:::

Next: [Episode 6 — Subsetting Data](r-06-subsetting-data.qmd).
